QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_37

question.text: Under Rule 17 of the Code of Conduct, which of the following constitutes "unfair competition" by a merchant banker?

answer.text: Offering lower management fees than competitors
Weaning away clients by assuring a higher premium or advantageous offer price
Hiring employees from competing merchant banks

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rule 17 specifically defines unfair competition as weaning away clients from competitors by giving assurances of a higher premium or more advantageous offer price. Such assurances compromise market integrity. **The Trap:** Offering lower management fees is normal competitive behavior and is not considered unfair competition under this rule. Advertising and hiring are legitimate business practices. **Cross-Exam Context:** Unfair competition concepts are also relevant in NISM Series X-A (Investment Adviser) and Series V-A (Mutual Fund) regarding distributor practices. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compete on service quality and track record, never on pricing promises — the market determines premiums, not the merchant banker.
